title: Officials: Man who shot 2 in Hampton Beach before dying by suicide was active-duty Navy sailor
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tyshawn Cooper, 28, an active-duty Navy sailor, shot two people in Hampton, NH, before dying by suicide.
The incident occurred on July 5 on Ocean Boulevard; the injured, a 23-year-old man and 25-year-old woman, are hospitalized.
Cooper, an Information Systems Technician with the USS Hampton, was assigned to the Portsmouth Naval Shipyard in Kittery, ME.

title: Report: Celtics’ Paul George turned down $3.9 million trade bonus
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul George waived his $3.9 million trade bonus, keeping his salary cap hit at $54.1 million with the Boston Celtics.
The Celtics, $30 million over the salary cap, have $25 million in space before the first luxury tax apron.
Avoiding the second luxury tax apron is crucial for the Celtics to evade severe penalties detailed by the NBA.

title: R.I. food bank thanks Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce for pre-wedding donation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated $1 million to the Rhode Island Community Food Bank before their July 3 wedding.
The donation will help the food bank purchase nutritious, culturally appropriate food and support its 137 state-wide agencies.
The couple's other donations included $2 million to Feeding America and $1 million to Harvesters food bank.

title: 5 takeaways from the Celtics’ press conference about the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to Philadelphia for Paul George and draft assets, citing the new financial landscape.
President Brad Stevens emphasized the need for flexibility due to salary cap constraints under the new collective bargaining rules.
Stevens clarified that Jayson Tatum had no input in the decision to trade Brown, maintaining a rule of non-involvement.

title: 5 takeaways from the U.S. loss to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The U.S. lost to Belgium 4-1 in the World Cup Round of 16 in Seattle, ending their tournament run.
Belgium's Charles De Ketelaere scored twice, capitalizing on U.S. defensive errors during the match.
The U.S. failed to capitalize on home advantage, with defensive lapses and ineffective attack play contributing to their defeat.

title: 2 siblings drown in Vermont river, brother tried to save younger sister, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sandro Lala, 25, and Yazmin Yupangui, 11, drowned in Lamoille River, Vermont while fishing with family.
Yazmin fell into the water; Sandro attempted a rescue but both vanished in the river's strong current.
Bodies were recovered after storm hindered search; GoFundMe raised nearly $70,000 for funeral expenses.

title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston officials cut over 560 positions from the Boston Public Schools due to budget constraints and enrollment declines.
City rejected using reserve funds to prevent school layoffs, focusing instead on long-term financial stability and immediate community needs.
Of 368 tenured Boston Teachers Union positions eliminated, 311 have been reassigned, with remaining educators expected to be placed by summer's end.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Harmful bacteria levels in Merrimack River remain high after a sewer overflow, affecting recreational and shellfish harvesting areas.
Four out of seven testing sites showed fecal coliform and E. coli levels above recreational limits; Plum Island had the highest readings.
Massachusetts orders emergency shellfish closures in several towns; the Division of Marine Fisheries is increasing sample testing.

title: Boater remains missing after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three people were involved in a boat crash near Salisbury Beach on Friday night; two were rescued and hospitalized.
49-year-old Vilmar Dorner from North Reading remains missing after the crash, according to relatives.
Search efforts by Massachusetts State Police and the U.S. Coast Guard were unsuccessful; the Coast Guard suspended the search Saturday.

title: After officiating Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding, Adam Sandler played pickup basketball in this Mass. vacation hotspot
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Adam Sandler officiated Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding at Madison Square Garden on July 3.
Sandler played pickup basketball on Nantucket on July 5, asking police for game locations.
Sandler's basketball skills impressed local players, who described him as a friendly and skilled participant.
